task_text: A biology teacher combines taxonomy and maths.
part1_text: How many chambers does the human heart have?
part2_text: Average heart rate 72 bpm. Beats in 8 hours?

task_text: A science journalist covers astronomy.
part1_text: Which planet is closest to the Sun?
part2_text: Mercury orbits the Sun in 88 Earth days. How many Mercury years in one Earth year (to 1 decimal place)?

task_text: A project manager tracks budget and drafts memos.
part1_text: Monthly budget $54,000 divided equally among 12 departments. Each department's allocation?
part2_text: Rewrite in passive voice: 'The team submitted the report.'
answer_b: The report was submitted by the team.

task_text: A sales manager reviews revenue and drafts client communications.
part1_text: 25 items sold at $18.50 each. Total revenue?
part2_text: Antonym of 'verbose'? A) Loquacious  B) Concise  C) Elaborate  D) Wordy

task_text: An accountant reviews payroll.
part1_text: 45 employees earn $22/hr and work 40 hrs/week. Weekly payroll?
part2_text: If any employee earns overtime (>40 hrs), it must be approved. No overtime hours logged. Is approval needed?

task_text: A production manager tracks output and quality rules.
part1_text: Line produces 240 units/hr. 6-hour shift. Defect rate 3%. Good units produced?
part2_text: Rule: if defect rate > 5%, halt the line. Current rate 3%. Halt the line?

task_text: A nurse calculates infusion rates and follows administration protocol.
part1_text: IV bag: 1,000 mL to be infused over 8 hours. Rate in mL per hour?
part2_text: Medication administration: (1) check patient ID (2) verify order (3) prepare dose (4) administer (5) document. Nurse administers then documents. Is this acceptable if all prior steps were followed?

task_text: A project manager calculates budget burn and follows approval procedures.
part1_text: Monthly budget $80,000. Spent: 42% in month 1 and 31% in month 2. Total spent after two months?
part2_text: Approval procedure: (1) draft request (2) cost estimate (3) manager sign-off (4) finance approval (5) execution. Request executed after steps 1–3, skipping step 4. Protocol followed?

task_text: An office manager plans a team event and handles social dynamics.
part1_text: Team lunch for 15 people at $22 each plus an 18% tip. Total cost?
part2_text: A new team member rarely speaks in meetings and gives short answers. Most likely interpretation? A) Rude  B) Shy or adapting to team norms  C) Disinterested  D) Hostile

task_text: An HR manager handles salary reviews and team culture.
part1_text: Annual raise of 8% on a $52,500 salary. New salary?
part2_text: A colleague publicly criticises your work without prior discussion. Best professional response? A) Argue back immediately  B) Request a private conversation later  C) Ignore it  D) Escalate to HR immediately

task_text: An engineer calculates material needs.
part1_text: Pipe costs $18.50/m; 45 m needed. Total cost?
part2_text: Pipes form an L: 20 m east then 25 m north. Straight-line distance from start to end (nearest whole m)?

task_text: A store manager tracks sales and shelving.
part1_text: Mon $1,240 + Tue $985 + Wed $1,103. Three-day total?
part2_text: Products on a 5-col × 3-row shelf. A shipment fills bottom-left 2×2. Occupied slots?

task_text: A project manager tracks budget and deadlines.
part1_text: Budget $50,000. Spent $12,750 wk1 and $18,430 wk2. Remaining?
part2_text: Project starts March 1, finish in 90 days. Deadline (month and day)?

task_text: An office manager handles scheduling and payroll.
part1_text: Employee earns $28.50/hr, works 37.5 hrs this week. Gross earnings?
part2_text: Meeting scheduled Tuesday 2:00 PM, postponed +2 days +3 hours. New day and time?

task_text: A cultural journalist writes about art and distinguishes word meanings.
part1_text: What is an 'allusion'? A) A false belief  B) An indirect reference  C) A type of metaphor  D) A synonym
part2_text: Who painted the Mona Lisa?

task_text: A science journalist writes about biology and uses precise grammar.
part1_text: Which sentence uses the subjunctive correctly? A) 'I suggest that he attends the meeting.'  B) 'I suggest that he attend the meeting.'  C) 'I suggested that he attended the meeting.'  D) 'I suggest he is attending.'
part2_text: How many bones are in the adult human body?

task_text: A science educator applies deductive reasoning and recalls biology facts.
part1_text: All noble gases are chemically inert. Helium is a noble gas. Is helium chemically inert?
part2_text: What is the most abundant gas in Earth's atmosphere?

task_text: A history teacher applies logical analysis to historical facts.
part1_text: Democracy requires free elections. Country X holds free elections. Does Country X necessarily have a democracy?
part2_text: What year was the United Nations founded?

task_text: A chemistry teacher covers reaction facts and lab safety procedures.
part1_text: What gas is produced when baking soda and vinegar react?
part2_text: Lab safety procedure: (1) put on goggles (2) put on gloves (3) conduct experiment (4) clean up spills (5) dispose of chemicals. Student conducts experiment before wearing gloves. Protocol followed?

task_text: A chemistry teacher covers reaction facts and lab safety procedures.
part1_text: What gas is produced when baking soda and vinegar react?
part2_text: Lab safety procedure: (1) put on goggles (2) put on gloves (3) conduct experiment (4) clean up spills (5) dispose of chemicals. Student conducts experiment before wearing gloves. Protocol followed?

task_text: A journalist covers social dynamics and history.
part1_text: A person gives a gift and the recipient opens it without comment. In most Western contexts, is this polite?
part2_text: Who wrote 'Pride and Prejudice'?

task_text: An educator teaches history and classroom dynamics.
part1_text: Which empire built the Colosseum in Rome? A) Greek  B) Ottoman  C) Roman  D) Byzantine
part2_text: A student always answers first and loudly, even when wrong. Others stop raising hands. What norm is disrupted?

task_text: An architect reviews historical building dimensions and facts.
part1_text: A rectangular tower footprint: 45m × 30m. Floor area in m²?
part2_text: In which city is the Eiffel Tower located?

task_text: An architect reviews historical building dimensions and facts.
part1_text: A rectangular tower footprint: 45m × 30m. Floor area in m²?
part2_text: In which city is the Eiffel Tower located?

task_text: A geography teacher covers time zones and world facts.
part1_text: Flight departs London at 9:00 AM GMT, arrives New York at 11:30 AM local time (EST, UTC−5). Flight duration?
part2_text: What is the longest river in the world?

task_text: A history teacher combines timeline calculations with factual recall.
part1_text: World War I began in 1914 and ended in 1918. Duration in years?
part2_text: Who was the first President of the United States?

task_text: A contract manager checks compliance and drafts language.
part1_text: Clause: 'Payment due within 30 days OR extension granted.' Client unpaid 35 days, no extension. Clause violated?
part2_text: Rewrite as active: 'The completion of the audit by the team occurred on Friday.'
answer_b: The team completed the audit on Friday.

task_text: A software architect reviews logic and documentation.
part1_text: Function returns True if A AND (B OR C). A=True, B=False, C=True. Return value?
part2_text: Antonym of 'deprecated'? A) Obsolete  B) Current  C) Supported  D) Removed

task_text: A publisher follows manuscript handling steps and checks editorial style.
part1_text: Which is the correct spelling? A) Neccessary  B) Necessary  C) Necessery  D) Necesary
part2_text: Manuscript handling: (1) submission (2) editorial assessment (3) peer review (4) revision (5) acceptance. Manuscript accepted before peer review. Protocol followed?

task_text: A communications manager follows document approval procedures and checks grammar.
part1_text: Identify the grammatical error: 'Each of the team members are responsible for their own tasks.'
part2_text: Approval process: (1) write (2) edit (3) legal review (4) sign-off (5) distribute. Document distributed without legal review. Protocol followed?

task_text: A team leader drafts meeting notes and reads team dynamics.
part1_text: What is the passive voice version of: 'The team exceeded all targets'?
part2_text: In a meeting, one member dominates and others fall silent. What group dynamic is occurring? A) Synergy  B) Brainstorming  C) Social loafing  D) Social silencing
answer_a: All targets were exceeded by the team.

task_text: An HR professional writes policies and observes team culture.
part1_text: Which is a euphemism for 'layoffs'? A) Rightsizing  B) Promotion  C) Expansion  D) Recruitment
part2_text: An employee receives positive feedback but immediately deflects credit to their team. What social behaviour does this best represent? A) Insecurity  B) Modesty and collaborative culture  C) Poor communication  D) Incompetence

task_text: An interior designer plans room dimensions and writes briefs.
part1_text: Room dimensions: 5.5m × 4.2m. Floor area in m²?
part2_text: What is the plural of 'analysis'?

task_text: An interior designer plans room dimensions and writes briefs.
part1_text: Room dimensions: 5.5m × 4.2m. Floor area in m²?
part2_text: What is the plural of 'analysis'?

task_text: A quality engineer follows testing protocols.
part1_text: Protocol: (1) calibrate (2) sample (3) test (4) log (5) review. Step 3 was done before step 2. Protocol violated?
part2_text: Rule: log only tested samples. An untested sample was logged. Rule violated?

task_text: A security officer follows access control procedures.
part1_text: Steps: (1) scan badge (2) enter PIN (3) pass biometrics. A door opened after steps 1 and 2 without step 3. All steps completed?
part2_text: Policy: biometric check is mandatory. A person entered without it. Policy satisfied?

task_text: A manager applies promotion criteria and manages expectations.
part1_text: Promotion criteria: 3+ years experience AND exceed targets in at least 2 consecutive years. Candidate has 4 years experience but exceeded targets only once. Eligible?
part2_text: The candidate learns they were not promoted despite meeting the experience requirement. Most likely emotional response? A) Satisfaction  B) Indifference  C) Frustration or disappointment  D) Relief

task_text: A negotiator checks agreement conditions and reads social dynamics.
part1_text: Agreement holds only if BOTH parties comply. One party complied; the other did not. Is the agreement in force?
part2_text: The non-compliant party claims ignorance of the terms. What social mechanism is this? A) Bad faith negotiation  B) Plausible deniability  C) Competitive bargaining  D) Escalation

task_text: A safety inspector checks occupancy limits for a room.
part1_text: Room: 6m × 4m with a 1m × 1m structural column. Net floor area in m²?
part2_text: Building code: minimum 4 m² net floor area per occupant. What is the maximum number of occupants allowed?

task_text: An engineer designs a cylindrical storage tank and checks regulations.
part1_text: Cylindrical tank: radius 3m, height 5m. Volume in m³ (π ≈ 3.14)?
part2_text: Regulation: tanks exceeding 150 m³ require an overflow valve. Based on your answer, does this tank require one?

task_text: An operations manager tracks maintenance schedules.
part1_text: Machine last serviced January 1. Must be serviced every 90 days. Next service due?
part2_text: If the next service falls in Q2 (April–June), a different crew handles it. Based on your answer, which crew?

task_text: A scheduler manages project timelines.
part1_text: Project kickoff: Monday Feb 3. Milestone A in 2 weeks. Milestone B: 3 weeks after A. Milestone B date?
part2_text: Rule: if Milestone B is before March 14, proceed to Phase 2. Based on your answer, proceed?

task_text: A supervisor handles a bystander situation and follows incident reporting procedures.
part1_text: An employee witnesses workplace harassment but says nothing fearing retaliation. What social phenomenon explains this? A) Bystander effect  B) Social loafing  C) Diffusion of responsibility  D) Both A and C
part2_text: Reporting procedure: (1) document incident (2) report to supervisor (3) HR review (4) investigation (5) outcome communication. Supervisor launches investigation before HR review. Protocol followed?

task_text: A facilitator runs a brainstorming workshop and follows group process steps.
part1_text: Participants in a brainstorming session start critiquing ideas as soon as they are raised. What brainstorming norm is being violated?
part2_text: Workshop procedure: (1) set ground rules (2) warm-up (3) brainstorm (4) prioritise (5) action planning. Group jumps from brainstorming directly to action planning. Protocol followed?

task_text: A warehouse manager calculates pallet capacity and follows stock protocols.
part1_text: Warehouse floor: 30m × 20m. Each pallet has a footprint of 1.2m × 1m. How many pallets fit (floor area only)?
part2_text: Protocol: (1) receive goods (2) scan items (3) quality check (4) assign shelf (5) shelve. A shipment is shelved before scanning. Protocol followed?

task_text: An interior designer measures furniture footprints and checks the design process.
part1_text: Furniture footprints: sofa 3m × 1m, dining table 2m × 1m, coffee table 1.2m × 0.8m. Combined footprint area in m²?
part2_text: Design process: (1) client brief (2) concept design (3) client approval (4) procurement (5) installation. Designer begins procurement before receiving client approval. Process correctly followed?

task_text: A factory supervisor manages processes and scheduling.
part1_text: Quality checks start 7:00 AM every 90 minutes. Time of the 5th check?
part2_text: Protocol: (1) visual inspection (2) weight check (3) electrical test (4) packaging. Shipment skips step 3. Protocol followed?

task_text: A factory supervisor manages processes and scheduling.
part1_text: Quality checks start 7:00 AM every 90 minutes. Time of the 5th check?
part2_text: Protocol: (1) visual inspection (2) weight check (3) electrical test (4) packaging. Shipment skips step 3. Protocol followed?

task_text: A school principal plans classroom seating arrangements.
part1_text: Classroom: 10m × 8m. Teacher's area at the front takes a 10m × 1.5m strip. Remaining student area in m²?
part2_text: Students in rows perform worse on collaborative tasks than students seated in clusters. Most likely explanation?
answer_b: row seating inhibits peer interaction and collaboration

task_text: A school principal plans classroom seating arrangements.
part1_text: Classroom: 10m × 8m. Teacher's area at the front takes a 10m × 1.5m strip. Remaining student area in m²?
part2_text: Students in rows perform worse on collaborative tasks than students seated in clusters. Most likely explanation?
answer_b: row seating inhibits peer interaction and collaboration

task_text: An event coordinator manages event timing and guest relations.
part1_text: Gala dinner: doors open 6:30 PM, event closes 11:00 PM. Total event duration in hours?
part2_text: The keynote speaker arrives 25 minutes late. What social norm was most directly violated?
answer_b: punctuality or respect for others' time

task_text: A team lead manages a deadline and team communication.
part1_text: Project deadline: Friday 5:00 PM. Current time: Wednesday 11:30 AM. Hours remaining?
part2_text: A team member frequently misses deadlines. How should a manager first respond? A) Immediate termination  B) Issue a formal warning  C) Have a private conversation to understand the cause  D) Public criticism in a team meeting

task_text: A construction manager tracks layout and schedule.
part1_text: Building footprint 40m × 25m. Setback rule: 5m on all sides. Buildable area?
part2_text: Permit processing takes 6 weeks. Applied March 10. Permit available from?

task_text: A games designer plans a board layout and production schedule.
part1_text: Game board is 60cm × 60cm divided into a 10×10 grid. Each cell area in cm²?
part2_text: Design phase: 3 weeks. Testing: 2 weeks. Manufacturing: 6 weeks. Launch 4 weeks after manufacturing. Total time from design start to launch?
